content.problemStatement:
Chef has two integers XXX and SSS. He is interested in sequences of non-negative integers such that the sum of their elements is SSS and the bitwise OR of their elements is XXX. Chef would like to know the shortest possible length such a sequence can have.

Formally, find the smallest positive integer NNN such that there exists a sequence A=[A1,A2,…,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1​,A2​,…,AN​] satisfying the following conditions:


Each AiA_iAi​ is a non-negative integer
A1+A2+…+AN=SA_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_N = SA1​+A2​+…+AN​=S
A1∨A2∨…∨AN=XA_1 \vee A_2 \vee \ldots \vee A_N = XA1​∨A2​∨…∨AN​=X
where ∨\vee∨ denotes the bitwise OR operation.

If there does not exist any sequence which satisfies the above conditions, output −1-1−1. Otherwise, output the **minimum** possible length of such a sequence.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers XXX and SSS — the required bitwise OR and sum, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer — the shortest possible length of such a sequence, or −1-1−1 if no such sequence exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: One of the valid arrays of length 333 is [9,9,5][9,9,5][9,9,5]. It can be shown that there is no valid array with length <3\lt 3<3.
Test Case 222: There does not exist any sequence whose sum is 131313 and bitwise OR is 666.
Test Case 333: The valid array of minimum length contains 252525 ones.
